ogTitle: Tol Tol vs Tom Groggin: Climate Safety

heading: Tol Tol vs Tom Groggin — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tol Tol, Victoria and Tom Groggin, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tom Groggin has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Tol Tol (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tol Tol or Tom Groggin safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tol Tol has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Tom Groggin scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tol Tol scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Tom Groggin scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tol Tol or Tom Groggin?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tom Groggin has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
